hero.title: PDF Merger
hero.tagline: Drop and Merge - Combine multiple PDF files into a single document in seconds. Upload your files, drag to reorder them, then merge and download the result.

hint.text: Do you need to split them before re-merge?

howMergeWorks.heading: How PDF merge works
howMergeWorks.body: A PDF merge operation takes the page streams from each source document in the order you specify and writes them sequentially into a new PDF container. The resulting file shares no data with the originals — each page is fully reproduced, preserving text, images, annotations, and vector graphics.

howSizeDetermined.heading: How merged size is determined
howSizeDetermined.body: The merged PDF's size is roughly the sum of the input files' sizes, plus a small amount of overhead for the new cross-reference table and trailer. Duplicate embedded resources (fonts, images) are not automatically de-duplicated unless the tool explicitly does so.

whyUseOnline.heading: Why use an online merger
whyUseOnline.body: Browser-based merging eliminates the need to install software. Files are transferred to a short-lived server process, merged, and returned; the originals remain on your device.

bestPractices.heading: Best practices
bestPractices.item1: Merge in order — arrange your files beforehand to avoid re-merging.
bestPractices.item2: Compress after merging — use the checkbox to reduce the size of the final file.
bestPractices.item3: Check file count — most online tools handle up to a reasonable number of files.

whatItDoes.heading: What it does
whatItDoes.item1: Combines multiple PDFs into a single ordered document.

usefulWhen.heading: Useful when
usefulWhen.item1: Combining chapters or sections written separately.
usefulWhen.item2: Merging scanned pages saved as individual files.
usefulWhen.item3: Combining a cover page, main document, and appendix.

comparison.heading: Comparison
comparison.item1: Unlike desktop tools, this runs in your browser without installation.

privacy.heading: Privacy & retention
privacy.body: Uploaded files are processed on short-lived infrastructure and removed according to the app's retention policy. If you are handling highly sensitive documents, prefer local desktop tools or an on-prem solution that matches your compliance requirements.

faq.heading: FAQs
faq.a1: A: Files are processed temporarily and auto-deleted according to the app's policy.
faq.q2: Q: Will merging change the formatting of individual PDFs?
faq.a2: A: No — pages are reproduced as-is; formatting is preserved.
faq.q3: Q: Can I control the order of pages?
faq.a3: A: Yes — drag files to reorder them before merging.
faq.q4: Q: Can I merge more than two PDFs?
faq.a4: A: Yes — there is no fixed limit, though very large batches may be slower.
faq.q5: Q: What if the result is larger than expected?
faq.a5: A: Enable compression after merging to reduce the final file size.

howItWorks.step1: Upload the PDF files you want to merge.
howItWorks.step2: Drag to reorder files to set the merge order.
howItWorks.step3: Click Merge to send files to the backend merging process.
howItWorks.step4: Download the merged PDF when processing completes.

dropZone.text: Drag and drop your PDF files here

fileList.title: PDF files ({{count}})
fileList.reorderHint: Drag files to reorder merge order.

note: Note: The compressed file will be stored in Cloudflare R2 storage for 30 min. Please download it within this period. After 30 min, the file will be automatically deleted.

lowerHint.text: Trouble merging because the files are too large?

guide.title: Combine Multiple PDFs Into One (Clean, Organized & Stress-Free Guide)
guide.intro: Working with multiple PDF files can get messy fast. Instead of sending or managing them one by one, you can merge everything into a single, clean document.
guide.cta: 👉 This guide shows you how to merge PDFs efficiently, organize them properly, and avoid common mistakes.

whatIs.heading: 📌 What Is a PDF Merger?
whatIs.body: A PDF merger is a tool that lets you:

whyMatters.heading: 🎯 Why Merging PDFs Matters
whyMatters.item1: Better Organization — turn several files into one structured document.
whyMatters.item2: Easier Sharing — one file is faster, cleaner, and less confusing.
whyMatters.item3: Professional Presentation — a single merged PDF looks polished.
whyMatters.item4: Improved Workflow — reduces clutter and repetitive tasks.

useCases.heading: 🛠 Common Use Cases
useCases.item1: 💼 Business Documents — combine reports, invoices, contracts
useCases.item2: 🎓 Student Assignments — merge multiple sections into one submission
useCases.item3: 🧾 Scanned Files — combine scanned pages into a single file
useCases.item4: 📑 Application Submissions — resume + cover letter + certificates

howWorks.heading: 🧠 How PDF Merging Works (Simple Explanation)
howWorks.body: Files are loaded, pages are extracted and arranged, and a new PDF is generated. The original files remain unchanged.

tips.heading: 🎨 Tips to Make Your Merged PDF Look Clean
tips.item2: ✅ Use clear file naming (rename files in correct order)
tips.item4: ✅ Check final output (page order, orientation, formatting)

safety.heading: 🔐 Is It Safe to Merge PDFs Online?
safety.body: Most tools process files temporarily and delete them after completion. Still, avoid uploading sensitive documents unless you trust the tool.

faq.heading: ❓ FAQ
faq.q1: Can I change page order after merging?
faq.a1: Yes — most tools allow reordering before final export.
faq.a2: No — good tools preserve layout and formatting.
faq.q3: Can I merge images into PDFs?
faq.a3: Yes — many tools support combining images and PDFs.
faq.q4: Is there a limit on number of files?
faq.a4: Depends on the tool, but many support multiple files easily.

conclusion.heading: 🧾 Conclusion
conclusion.body: Merging PDFs helps you stay organized and present information professionally. With the right approach you can turn scattered files into a clean document in seconds.

guide.ctaBtn: 👉 Try it here: PDF Merger Tool
